speaker: GAUNT
text: Alas, the part I had in Woodstock’s blood

speaker: GAUNT
text: Doth more solicit me than your exclaims

speaker: GAUNT
text: To stir against the butchers of his life.

speaker: GAUNT
text: But since correction lieth in those hands

speaker: GAUNT
text: Which made the fault that we cannot correct,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Put we our quarrel to the will of heaven,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Who, when they see the hours ripe on Earth,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Will rain hot vengeance on offenders’ heads.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Finds brotherhood in thee no sharper spur?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hath love in thy old blood no living fire?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Edward’s seven sons, whereof thyself art one,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Were as seven vials of his sacred blood

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or seven fair branches springing from one root.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Some of those seven are dried by nature’s course,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Some of those branches by the Destinies cut.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But Thomas, my dear lord, my life, my Gloucester,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: One vial full of Edward’s sacred blood,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: One flourishing branch of his most royal root,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Is cracked and all the precious liquor spilt,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Is hacked down, and his summer leaves all faded,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: By envy’s hand and murder’s bloody ax.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Ah, Gaunt, his blood was thine! That bed, that womb,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That metal, that self mold that fashioned thee

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Made him a man; and though thou livest and breathest,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yet art thou slain in him. Thou dost consent

speaker: DUCHESS
text: In some large measure to thy father’s death

speaker: DUCHESS
text: In that thou seest thy wretched brother die,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Who was the model of thy father’s life.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Call it not patience, Gaunt. It is despair.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: In suff’ring thus thy brother to be slaughtered,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou showest the naked pathway to thy life,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Teaching stern murder how to butcher thee.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That which in mean men we entitle patience

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Is pale, cold cowardice in noble breasts.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What shall I say? To safeguard thine own life,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The best way is to venge my Gloucester’s death.

speaker: GAUNT
text: God’s is the quarrel; for God’s substitute,

speaker: GAUNT
text: His deputy anointed in His sight,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Hath caused his death, the which if wrongfully

speaker: GAUNT
text: Let heaven revenge, for I may never lift

speaker: GAUNT
text: An angry arm against His minister.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Where, then, alas, may I complain myself?

speaker: GAUNT
text: To God, the widow’s champion and defense.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why then I will. Farewell, old Gaunt.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou goest to Coventry, there to behold

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Our cousin Hereford and fell Mowbray fight.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: O, sit my husband’s wrongs on Hereford’s spear,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That it may enter butcher Mowbray’s breast!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or if misfortune miss the first career,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Be Mowbray’s sins so heavy in his bosom

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That they may break his foaming courser’s back

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And throw the rider headlong in the lists,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: A caitiff recreant to my cousin Hereford!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Farewell, old Gaunt. Thy sometime brother’s wife

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With her companion, grief, must end her life.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Sister, farewell. I must to Coventry.

speaker: GAUNT
text: As much good stay with thee as go with me.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yet one word more. Grief boundeth where it falls,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Not with the empty hollowness, but weight.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I take my leave before I have begun,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For sorrow ends not when it seemeth done.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Commend me to thy brother, Edmund York.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Lo, this is all. Nay, yet depart not so!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Though this be all, do not so quickly go;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I shall remember more. Bid him—ah, what?—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With all good speed at Plashy visit me.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Alack, and what shall good old York there see

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But empty lodgings and unfurnished walls,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Unpeopled offices, untrodden stones?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And what hear there for welcome but my groans?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Therefore commend me; let him not come there

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To seek out sorrow that dwells everywhere.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Desolate, desolate, will I hence and die.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: The last leave of thee takes my weeping eye.
